item.title: Discovering Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This talk was pre┬şsent┬şed at Py┬şCon CZ 2017 and Eu┬şroPy┬şthon 2017. Here are the ref┬şer┬şences and re┬şsources for the pre┬şsen┬şta┬ştion.

The code used for the talk, is avail┬şable here and ┬şcon┬ştains some ex┬şpla┬şna┬ştions along with the ex┬şam┬şples.

Read moreÔÇŽ (1 min remaining to read)

item.title: The __set_name__ method for descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
De┬şscrip┬ştors gen┬şer┬şal┬şly have to in┬şter┬şact with at┬ştributes of the man┬şaged ob┬şjec┬şt, and this is done by in┬şspect┬şing __┬şdic┬şt__ on that ob┬şject (or call┬şing getat┬ştr/se┬ştat┬ştr, but the prob┬şlem is the same), and find┬şing the key un┬şder┬ş the spe┬şcif┬şic name.

For this rea┬şson, the de┬şscrip┬ştor will have to know the name of the key to look ┬şfor, which is re┬şlat┬şed to the name of the at┬ştribute is man┬şag┬şing.

On pre┬şvi┬şous ver┬şsions of Python this had to be done ex┬şplic┬şit┬şly. If we want┬şed to ┬şwork around it, there were some more ad┬şvanced ways to do so. Luck┬şi┬şly, af┬şter PEP-487 (added in Python 3.6), there are some en┬şhance┬şments re┬şgard┬şing class cre┬şation, which al┬şso af┬şfect┬şs de┬şscrip┬ştors.

Let's re┬şview the prob┬şlem, the pre┬şvi┬şous ap┬şproach┬şes to tack┬şle it, and the mod┬şern way of solv┬şing it.

Read moreÔÇŽ (4 min remaining to read)

item.title: Descriptors & Decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
De┬şscrip┬ştors are an amaz┬şing tool to have in our tool┬şbox, as they come in handy in many op┬şpor┬ştu┬şni┬şties.

Prob┬şa┬şbly the best thing about de┬şscrip┬ştors, is that they can im┬şprove oth┬şer ┬şso┬şlu┬ştion┬şs. Let's see how we can write bet┬şter dec┬şo┬şra┬ştors, by us┬şing de┬şscrip┬ştors.

Read moreÔÇŽ (5 min remaining to read)

item.title: Types of Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Re┬şsum┬şing from where we left of┬şf, on the pre┬şvi┬şous post, on which we took A first look at de┬şscrip┬ştors, it's time to ex┬şplore their dif┬şfer┬şent type┬şs and how they work in┬şter┬şnal┬şly.

In Python, almost everything is represented with a dictionary. Objects are dictionaries. Classes are objects, hence they also are contained into a dictionary. This is denoted by the __dict__ attribute that objects have.

There are two types of descriptors: data descriptors and non-data ones. If a descriptor implements both [1] __get__() and __set__(), it's called a data descriptor; otherwise is a non-data descriptor.

Read moreÔÇŽ (7 min remaining to read)

item.title: A first look at descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
De┬şscrip┬ştors are one of the most pow┬şer┬şful fea┬ştures of Python. The rea┬şson why they're so pow┬şer┬şful is be┬şcause they en┬şable us to con┬ştrol the core op┬şer┬şa┬ştions (get, set, delete) [1], of an at┬ştribute in a giv┬şen ob┬şjec┬şt, so that we can hook a par┬ştic┬şu┬şlar code, con┬ştrolled by us, in or┬şder to mod┬şi┬şfy, change, or ex┬ştend the o┬şrig┬şi┬şnal op┬şer┬şa┬ştion.

Read moreÔÇŽ (8 min remaining to read)
